steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage makes two claims: (1) translation apps have improved and are increasingly useful for simple tasks, and (2) despite this, richer communication — in business or meaningful personal exchanges — still depends on nuances and conversational patterns that apps fail to convey. The answer must capture both the improvement and the remaining limitation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — They have improved remarkably over time but remain insufficient to support the complexity called for in certain interactions.: matches both points — advancement acknowledged, limitation in complex interactions confirmed. ✓
  • Choice B — They are coming to be embraced by international tourists but are viewed with skepticism by many business professionals.: the passage never mentions tourists or professional skepticism — unsupported. ✗
  • Choice C — They are becoming simpler to use but are inconsistent in how comprehensively they cover different languages.: ease of use and language coverage are not discussed; the limitation is about nuance, not coverage. ✗
  • Choice D — They have gained impressive capabilities but continue to be widely viewed as inadequate for most practical purposes.: the passage says apps are useful for perfunctory tasks — so calling them inadequate for 'most practical purposes' overstates the limitation. ✗
That is the correct answer.
